title: Present Simple Tense
theory: S + V(s/es) for habits, facts, routines.
example: She goes to school every day.

title: Present Continuous Tense
theory: S + am/is/are + V-ing for actions happening now.

title: Present Perfect Tense
theory: S + have/has + V3 for experiences or past with present relevance.

title: Present Perfect Continuous Tense
theory: S + have/has been + V-ing for actions started in the past and still continuing.
example: She has been studying since morning.

title: Past Simple Tense
theory: S + V2 for actions completed in the past.

title: Past Continuous Tense
theory: S + was/were + V-ing for actions happening at a past time.
example: I was reading a book at 8 PM.

title: Past Perfect Tense
theory: S + had + V3 for actions before a point in the past.
example: She had left before I arrived.

title: Past Perfect Continuous Tense
theory: S + had been + V-ing to emphasize duration before something in the past.
example: He had been waiting for hours.

title: Future Simple Tense
theory: S + will + V for predictions, promises, or decisions made at the moment.

title: Future Continuous Tense
theory: S + will be + V-ing for actions in progress at a future time.
example: This time tomorrow, I will be traveling.

title: Future Perfect Tense
theory: S + will have + V3 for actions completed before a point in the future.
example: She will have finished the work by 5 PM.

title: Future Perfect Continuous Tense
theory: S + will have been + V-ing for actions continuing up to a point in the future.
example: By next year, I will have been working here for 10 years.
